response.justification: While Bogdan is a seasoned polyglot engineer with impressive credentials at Google and Meta, his background is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary stack throughout his career is TypeScript/Node.js/JavaScript, with DLang as a secondary focus. Java appears only briefly at Altom (robotic arms, mobile tooling) and NI (C/C++ for a C IDE), with no Kotlin, no Spring, no Hibernate, no Quarkus, and no JVM-depth evidence anywhere in the resume. The NI LabWindows work is the closest brush with IDE tooling, but it was C-based and over a decade ago. This role requires deep JVM expertise and enterprise Java ecosystem fluency that this candidate simply does not demonstrate.
strengths: 15+ years of progressive engineering experience up to Staff Engineer level at Google and Meta
Genuine developer tooling interest demonstrated at Google (MCP code generators, agentic infrastructure)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, open source, public talks and workshops
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin expertise — TypeScript/Node.js is the dominant stack throughout the entire career
Zero Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience — the enterprise Java ecosystem is entirely absent from the resume
No JVM-focused IDE tooling experience; NI LabWindows work was C/C++-based and peripheral to current requirements

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
Senior credentials and developer tooling interest
